title: pavetta phanerophlebia
intro: Pavetta phanerophlebia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the Indian spurge or Indian staff tree. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Pavetta phanerophlebia include relief from fever, pain, and inflammation, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and prevent cardiovascular disease. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Pavetta phanerophlebia are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed for their therapeutic benefits.
